title: Ocugen Announces FDA Clearance of OCU400 Phase 3 Clinical Trial - First Gene Therapy with Broad RP Indication
text: Ocugen Announces FDA Clearance of IND Amendment for OCU400 Phase 3 Clinical Trial. FDA Clearance: In April 2024, the FDA cleared Ocugen's Investigational New Drug (IND) amendment to initiate a Phase 3 clinical trial of OCU400 for retinitis pigmentosa (RP). OCU400 became the first gene therapy program to enter Phase 3 with a broad RP indication. Trial Design: Phase 3 study includes 150 participants total. One arm of 75 participants with RHO gene mutation. Other arm with 75 participants that are gene-agnostic. In each arm, participants randomized 2:1 to treatment group (2.5 x 10^10 vg/eye of OCU400) and untreated control group. Timeline: OCU400 remains on track for 2026 BLA approval target. First patient dosed in Phase 3 liMeliGhT clinical trial in June 2024. Health Canada approval to initiate Phase 3 trial in Canada in August 2024. Recent 2-Year Data: Among 9 patients treated in Phase 1/2 study evaluable at 1 and 2 years posttreatment, all 9 (100%) showed improved or preserved visual function compared to untreated fellow eyes at both time points.
